title: Piña Colada Chia Pudding
summary:
Check out this Healthy Teaching Kitchen recipe video featuring a refreshing Piña Colada Chia Pudding, made with nourishing ingredients like chia seeds, pineapple, and light coconut milk. This tropical-inspired treat is easy to prepare, rich in fiber, and perfect for breakfast, a snack, or a light dessert. 

Prep: 5-10 minutes | Chill time: 6 hrs or overnight | Total: 6 hrs 10 minutes | Yield: 4 servings | Serving Size: 1/2 cup

Ingredients:
1 cup canned light coconut milk
¼ cup chia seeds
1 tablespoon agave syrup
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1½ cups crushed pineapple, drained
2 tablespoons unsweetened shredded coconut

Directions:
1. In a medium bowl, whisk together coconut milk, chia seeds, agave syrup, and vanilla until well combined.
2. Cover bowl and refrigerate for 6 hour (or overnight).Then, stir chia seed pudding to avoid clumps.
3. Divide half of the crushed pineapple evenly into four small serving cups (about 2 tablespoons each).
4. Spoon chia pudding over the pineapple, then top with the remaining crushed pineapple.
5. Sprinkle each serving with ½ table spoon unsweetened shredded coconut and serve, or keep chilled until ready to eat. 

Recipe keeps up to 3 days in the fridge.

Recipe Notes:
•Right before serving, layer with pineapple purée. If preparing ahead, store the purée and chia pudding separately to avoid a watery layer on top.
•For a refreshing frozen twist, blend frozen pineapple with ½ cup frozen banana slices for extra creaminess.

Nutrition Facts Per Serving: Calories: 200 | Total Fat: 13g | Saturated Fat: 8g | Sodium: 35mg | Total Carbohydrate: 20g | Dietary Fiber: 5g | Protein: 3g

For more recipes, please visit https://www.nutrition.va.gov

title: No One-Size-Fits-All: VA Mental Health Treatment for Veterans
summary: When it comes to mental health care, there’s no one-size-fits-all approach. Among VA's evidence-based therapies, medication management, talk therapy, group sessions, and residential treatment, you can find the treatment combination that’s tailored to your needs and lifestyle. Talk with your VA provider to find what works for you.

title: VA Mental Health Care: Personalized Treatment for Every Veteran
summary: VA mental health care is built for every Veteran, whether you are just getting out of the military or are an older Veteran facing new challenges. Explore treatment options, learn about evidence-based therapies, find local VA resources, explore self-help tools, and see how to connect with peer support or professional care when you’re ready.

title: Policy Brief: Tracking Headwaters Management for Wildfire Resilience in California
summary: Headwater regions are a critical component of California’s water supply, but severe wildfire is threatening them. The state is dramatically shifting how it manages its headwaters, working to reduce wildfire hazard on one million acres annually. While there’s been good progress, some policy improvements could help.

title: The Rise of Remote Work: Effects on California's Labor Market
summary: The pandemic accelerated widespread adoption of remote work, which has transformed California's labor market. In this report, we look at four key job groups where remote work has become common—technology, finance and accounting, business and government operations, and sales and marketing. Our review traces recent weakness in the state's labor market for these jobs to how the rise of remote work has restructured where Californians work, where they choose to live, and where employers look to hire.

summary: Training Command launched Ground Reconnaissance Course as part of an updated Marine Reconnaissance training curriculum at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, April 27. GRC is now the initial course in the updated curriculum, which replaces Basic Reconnaissance Course with two new courses: GRC and Amphibious Reconnaissance Course. This change comes from analysis of reconnaissance training and support to Force Design initiatives, including Scout Platoons and multidomain operations.

title: Updated ISO Standards in 2026
summary:
For auditors of ISO standards focused on management systems, 2026 will go down as a memorable, landmark year. Revised editions of the following three core standards have either been published or will be soon: ISO 14001:2026, “Environmental management systems—Requirements with guidance for use” was officially released on April 15. title: Global ACI Unveils New Logo and Digital Presence
summary:
Global Accreditation Cooperation Inc. (Global ACI), the new single international accreditation organization, commenced operations on January 1, 2026. Global ACI is the international authority on the accreditation of laboratories, certification bodies, inspection bodies, proficiency testing providers, validation/verification bodies, reference material producers, and biobanks. title: Iran and the Secretary of the Navy | John Vick on NewsNation
summary:
CVA Executive Director John Vick joined NewsNation today to discuss the removal of Secretary of the Navy John Phelan by Secretary of War Pete Hegseth. He also explored what comes next in the conflict with Iran, and what an impending War Powers deadline in Congress could mean for the President.

summary:

summary:

title: SWALWELL EXPOSED: SEX SCANDAL & SPY TIES
summary: Eric Swalwell is facing explosive new allegations of sexual misconduct and explicit messages sent to former staffers — just as his political career spirals. From the revived Fang Fang Chinese spy scandal to ethics investigations, resignation pressure, and the sudden collapse of his gubernatorial ambitions, this episode breaks down what really happened and what it means for Congress.

title: CIA Insider: Why the Iran Ceasefire is Extremely Dangerous
summary:
Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis breaks down the fragile new ceasefire with Iran and why the negotiated terms should worry every American.

In this hard-hitting episode, Sam reveals:
• Why the current Iran ceasefire is far more dangerous than it looks
• Iran’s true intentions driven by radical religious beliefs
• The hidden nuclear weapons strategy that changes everything
• How civilian intelligence and military operators actually work together
• The incredible story of American airmen recovered from a hostile environment

From fragile diplomatic deals to high-stakes nuclear brinkmanship and real-world rescue operations — this is unfiltered insight from a career CIA veteran who has seen it all.

Timestamps:
00:00 Ceasefire with Iran
11:00 Nuclear Weapons and Strategy
17:29 Recovery of Airmen

Guest: Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis

title: CALIFRAUDIA: $250 BILLION FRAUD EXPOSED
summary:
The conversation delves into the rampant fraud and corruption in California, with a focus on voter fraud, criminal activities, obstruction of investigations, and the call for criminal investigations and prosecutions. It also explores the state controller's plan for transparency, federal funding, hospice care fraud, fiscal fraud, and the culture of fraud and criminal behavior in California.

Takeaways

  • Rampant fraud and corruption in California
  • Efforts to expose and combat fraud

  • 21:28 Hospice Care Fraud and Federal Involvement

title: Unmasking Terrorism: The Truth Behind Recent Attacks
summary:
The conversation covers the misrepresentation of events by the media, the impact of immigration policies on terrorist attacks, the clarity of law enforcement response, and the importance of individual preparedness in the face of threats. It emphasizes the need for accurate reporting, effective law enforcement, and personal vigilance in the current security landscape.

Takeaways

  • Media Misrepresentation
  • Law Enforcement Response
  • Individual Preparedness
